results.title: Kimi K3 Tech Blog: Open Frontier Intelligence
results.content:
Try Kimi

# Kimi K3: Open Frontier Intelligence

Try Kimi K3

Today, we are introducing Kimi K3 — our most capable model. Kimi K3 is a 2.8T-parameter model built on our Kimi Delta Attention and Attention Residuals, with native vision capabilities and a 1-million-token context window. It is the world's first open 3T-class model, designed for frontier intelligence across long-horizon coding, knowledge work, and reasoning.

While its overall performance still trails the most powerful proprietary models, Claude Fable 5 and GPT 5.6 Sol, Kimi K3 demonstrated frontier-level performance across our evaluation suite, consistently outperforming other tested models. [...] ## An Open 3T-Class Model

Kimi K3 is the first open model to reach 2.8 trillion parameters. It marks the latest step in Kimi's sustained push at the scaling frontier: for nine of the past twelve months, Kimi models have set the upper bound of open-model sizes. [...] Kimi K3 is available today on Kimi.com, Kimi Work, Kimi Code, and the Kimi API. At launch, Kimi K3 will use max thinking effort by default, with low- and high-effort modes to be introduced in subsequent updates. We are currently working closely with inference partners and open-source maintainers to align technical details and ensure a reliable rollout across the ecosystem. The full model weights will be released by July 27, 2026. Further details on the architecture, training, and evaluations will be released alongside the Kimi K3 technical report.

## An Open 3T-Class Model

results.title: Kimi K3 shows open AI models have finally caught up with ...
results.content:
Open weights AI models have spent the past few years trying to close the gap with proprietary frontier models from OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google. Moonshot AI, a Beijing-based AI startup, now claims that its new Kimi K3 model has reached that frontier level and, in a few benchmarks, gone beyond it.

Kimi K3 is a 2.8-trillion-parameter Mixture-of-Experts model with native vision support and a context window of up to one million tokens. Moonshot AI describes it as the first open model in the three-trillion-parameter class. Despite its massive size, the model only activates 16 of its 896 experts for each token. This makes inference more efficient than running the full model for every request. [...] Of course, benchmark results do not always translate directly into a great real-world experience. In some of the aforementioned benchmarks, the models were tested using different agent harnesses, making direct comparisons less straightforward.

Kimi K3 is now available through Kimi’s web and desktop apps, Kimi Code, and its API. Pricing is $0.30/MTok for cache-hit input, $3.00/MTok for cache-miss input, and $15.00/MTok for output. Moonshot AI plans to release the full model weights by July 27, 2026.

# Kimi K3 shows open AI models have finally caught up with proprietary US-based rivals

##### The Kimi K3 model trails Claude Fable 5 and GPT-5.6 Sol in day-to-day general experience, but sets state-of-the-art records across several benchmarks.

Pradeep Viswanathan Neowin @pradeepviswav ·   with 0 comments

results.title: Kimi K3 intensifies competition between open and closed ...
results.content:
Skip to content

Kimi K3 intensifies competition between open and closed AI models 

The Chinese AI company Moonshot AI has announced Kimi K3, a large language model with 2.8 trillion parameters that will be released as an open model later this month. With this move, the company is taking a new step in the competition between open and closed AI models. According to Moonshot, K3 is the first open model to reach the threshold of nearly three trillion parameters. [...] Kimi K3 was developed for complex tasks such as software development, research, and agent-based AI workflows. The model features a context window of one million tokens, enabling it to process large amounts of code or documents in a single session. Upon its launch, the model will be available through Moonshot AI’s own services, while the model weights will be released to developers and researchers by July 27 at the latest. [...] With K3, Moonshot AI is explicitly targeting the high end of the AI market, where OpenAI and Anthropic currently set the standard with their closed models. The company acknowledges that Kimi K3 still lags behind models such as GPT-5.6 Sol and Claude Fable 5 in overall performance, but states that the model now achieves comparable results on various benchmarks and outperforms other open models.

The announcement underscores a broader trend in the AI market. Whereas the most powerful models were long available exclusively as closed commercial services, open models that fall into the same performance category are appearing with increasing frequency. This enables companies and research institutions to run and customize advanced AI models themselves.

## Designed for complex AI tasks

What an incredible day, cuz Moonshot AI is back with one of the most impressive model launches of the year, Kimi K3, an open frontier intelligence model. The Kimi K3 is a 2.8 trillion parameter open-source model with a 1 million token context window. It is natively multimodal, but that's not even the best part. It's currently ranked third overall on the world of AI benchmark, putting it in the same conversation as Claude Fable 5 and GPT 5.6 Soul, officially joining the current top tier of frontier models, which is just mind-boggling. The K3 is built for long-horizon agentic coding and self-evolving workflows. It outperforms Claude Opus 4.8 on coding across every benchmark.
